parsed_review.summary:
CP00-899 is a descriptor-only HRX bridge pack (RP30.P00.M09.S02–RP30.P01.M01.S02, risk A, 10 units) that carries the embedded HRX decision/review tail into the RP30 P01 Employee and EmploymentProfile domain foundation. Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob; no commands executed.

Verified consistency across the full chain: the live closeout-pack plan (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json), manifest (plan_binding_snapshot + included_units), registry binding (HRX_CP899_PACK_BINDING / HRX_CP899_REQUIREMENTS), and the generated contract (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json) all agree on pack id, risk class, unit count, range, micro-title distribution (decision:1, review:2, Employee:5, EmploymentProfile:2), and forward pointers (next CP00-900 / RP30.P01.M01.S03). validators.js enforces per-row boundaries and Employee/EmploymentProfile-specific constraints; I directly inspected the stored latest_projection rows and confirmed they match the generated descriptor (decision/review rows entity_name=HRXBaselineBoundary; Employee rows employee_entity_descriptor=true/entity_name=Employee; EmploymentProfile rows employment_profile_references_employee=true and employment_profile_references_user_account=false; domain_entities Employee.user_account=false / may_authorize_user_session=false, EmploymentProfile.references_user_account_directly=false). Tests in packages/hrx/test/model.test.js cover CP899 binding, coverage, descriptor, and contract projection. All mandatory artifacts exist; all cited npm scripts exist in package.json; README uses descriptor-only language with no over-claim.

No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold at row, descriptor, and HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION layers: descriptor_only, no runtime, no payroll runtime, no HR AI final judgment, no permission/audit/product-state writes, no runtime receipts, no real employee/candidate/payroll/client data, no credentials/secrets, no separate HRX product, Employee/User separation preserved, EmploymentProfile linked to Employee (not a user account).

Authority boundary respected: claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. Hermes pattern is used only for deterministic evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No over-claim: manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; the production_ready_flag is the gate name and construction-inspection shows it false pending review. Closeout gating additionally requires adjudication, queue regeneration, and the full deterministic validation ladder, so Claude review is necessary but not the sole/final gate.

Review-evidence hardening (CP178+): readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte stdout, valid JSON result, 0-byte stderr); review-request constrains output to JSON + schema with tools Read/Grep/Glob and no forbidden tools.

Provenance/limits: read-only review by static inspection only. command-evidence.json records targeted HRX validations (node --check, node --test, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) at exit_code 0 and explicitly defers the full deterministic ladder (closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) to post-review — expected sequencing, not a review-blocking gap. I confirmed the code and data those commands check are internally consistent but did not re-run them. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, .docx) are correctly excluded from pack scope; no active untracked implementation files omitted. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
